text: Hello and welcome to Real Easy English,
the podcast where we have real conversations in easy English to help you learn.
I'm Beth. And I'm Neil. There is a video version of this podcast on our website
where you can also find a worksheet and transcript to help you learn. Head to bbclearningenglish .com.
Hi Neil, how are you? I'm hungry. Me too.
I'm starving. Well, that's good because we're talking about lunch today.
We're recording this just before lunch, so it's no wonder that we're both very hungry.
You might be able to hear our tummies rumble because we're hungry.
OK, so shall we begin? Yes, let's do it. What have you got for lunch today?
for my lunch, I have chicken
with potatoes and vegetables in a harissa paste. Oh, that sounds really nice.
Yeah, it's leftovers actually, which I have heated up and brought in in a little flask.
What are leftovers? Good question. Leftovers.
That's food from a meal that you've already had that you didn't finish and you can heat it up and eat it again.
Yes. I also have leftovers for my lunch today.
So yesterday I made a pasta
for lunch with vegetables in it and I have that.
So Beth, what do you usually have for lunch? Is it usually leftovers or something else?
It's usually leftovers when I come into work
because it's easier and then I don't need to buy lunch.
But if I go out and buy food, then
I would usually go for something like sushi or ramen.
What about you? What do you usually have for lunch? Yeah, sometimes I have leftovers that I bring in.
Sometimes I buy a sandwich, for example. What's your favourite kind of sandwich?
If I have a sandwich, if I buy a sandwich for lunch,
I like to get things like smoked salmon, nice, with pickles maybe. Yeah.
There's a really good baguette I like actually that has smoked salmon and egg mayonnaise in it.
Oh, it's so nice. Stop it. Sorry. I'm so hungry.
Me too. Neil, do you ever skip lunch?
No way. I would be in a horrible mood if I skipped lunch.
I don't understand how anyone can skip any meal.
I need to eat all my meals yeah otherwise you get hangry
exactly hangry is hungry and angry
I'm on the I'm almost getting hangry now
what's your favorite meal is it lunch breakfast dinner
I think the evening meal is probably my favorite because we sit at the table and chat
and it's the biggest nicest meal of the day and you don't really have to do anything after so you can relax
yeah that's good i i really like brunch which is that sort of in between
breakfast and lunch maybe 10 o 'clock and i might have eggs and bacon and hash browns
and avocado a big meal full of lots of good food like a brunch do you ever have a working lunch
i do occasionally if i'm working from home um
because i can carry on working and eat quickly and then in my actual lunch break i might have
a nice long walk or something like that but when i'm in the office i don't want to eat at my desk.
No, me neither. I don't really like working lunches.
I would rather eat my lunch without working. Yeah, I'd prefer the same really.
Let's recap the language we heard during the conversation.
We had skip, which means not have have or do. For example, I never skip lunch.
We heard working lunch, which is when you eat your lunch while you're working.
We also had leftovers, which is when you don't finish a meal so you have more of it at a different time, like the next day.
And we talked about which meals and food we prefer.
If you prefer something, you like it better than something else.
Yes, and another phrase we can use to say this is would rather.
For example, I'd rather have sushi than a sandwich for lunch.
That's it for this episode of Real Easy English. Test what you've learned with a worksheet on our website bbclearningenglish .com.
And make sure you join us again for another Easy English conversation.
We'll be back next week. See you soon. Goodbye.
